title: Menu
description: Menu provides dropdown, split-button, and plain button triggers plus standalone menu lists with sections, shortcuts, submenus, and overlay behavior for dense command surfaces.
intro: Use `ui-menu` for compact action menus, overflow controls, contextual actions, and split-button patterns. Use `ui-menu-list` when you need to render the list surface itself, for example in custom overlays or embedded command panels.
importCode: import { MenuComponent, MenuListComponent, type MenuItem, type MenuSection } from 'ui';

featureSections.title: Basic menu button
featureSections.description: Start with a single dropdown trigger when a compact button needs to reveal a small action set.

featureSections.title: Dropdown, split, and button triggers
featureSections.description: Choose the trigger based on intent: dropdown for secondary actions, split for one clear primary action plus alternatives, and button when there is no menu at all.

featureSections.title: Sections and shortcuts
featureSections.description: Structured menu lists work well for editor-style and desktop-like command surfaces where grouping and shortcut hints improve scanning speed.

featureSections.title: Nested submenu flows
featureSections.description: Submenus fit insertion, export, and format trees where one top-level action branches into a small, well-scoped second level.

featureSections.title: Contextual row actions
featureSections.description: Per-row menus are one of the most common production uses of this component. Keep them short and focused on actions users expect in place.

featureSections.title: Overlay behavior in constrained shells
featureSections.description: Menus often live inside clipped cards, drawers, and scroll regions. The overlay should still escape the local layout cleanly.

featureSections.title: Workspace command header
featureSections.description: A realistic menu setup often appears beside search and primary actions in a compact command header rather than as an isolated button.

accessibility.title: Trigger and menu semantics
accessibility.body: `ui-menu` exposes a button-like trigger. When it opens a menu, the trigger reflects `aria-haspopup="menu"` and `aria-expanded`. `ui-menu-list` renders the actual menu surface with `role="menu"`.

accessibility.title: Keyboard behavior
accessibility.body:
Keyboard handling matters most inside `ui-menu-list`.

| Key | Action |
| --- | --- |
| ArrowDown / ArrowUp | Move focus between enabled menu rows. |
| Enter / Space | Activate the focused row. |
| ArrowRight | Open a submenu when the focused row has nested items. |
| ArrowLeft | Close the current submenu level. |
| Escape | Close the current menu surface. |
| Tab | Closes the menu instead of tabbing through every row. |

accessibility.title: Labels and split actions
accessibility.body: Icon-only triggers need a clear `ariaLabel`. For split buttons, the primary action and the dropdown affordance serve different purposes, so both need understandable labeling and should not duplicate hidden meaning.

apiSections.title: MenuComponent - ui-menu
apiSections.lead: Standalone trigger component for dropdown, split-button, and plain-button command patterns. It renders the trigger and manages the overlay-backed menu list.

blocks.title: Key inputs
rows: Native button type used for trigger buttons.
Semantic styling variant for the trigger.
Variant passed to nested menu rows.
Appearance passed to nested menu rows.
Optional shortcut hint shown in the trigger.
Items rendered in the overlay menu.
Optional max height for the rendered menu list.
Internal flag used when the menu is rendered as a nested submenu.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emits when a menu row is activated.
Emits when the overlay menu opens.
Emits when the overlay menu closes.
Emits when the primary half of a split trigger or a plain button trigger is activated.

apiSections.title: MenuListComponent - ui-menu-list
apiSections.lead: Standalone menu surface component used by `ui-menu` overlays and available directly for embedded command lists or custom overlay flows.

blocks.title: Key inputs and models
rows: Grouped menu sections with optional headers and dividers.
Flat menu item list used when sections are not provided.
Optional max height for the scrollable menu surface.
Whether the menu surface should render.
Default row variant applied to items.
Default row appearance applied to items.
{ id, label, icon?, shortcut?, disabled?, selected?, submenuItems?, action?, submenuAction?, type? }
Menu row contract used for buttons, submenu rows, and split rows.
Grouping contract for sectioned menu lists.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emits when a row action is executed.
Emits when a submenu row opens.
Emits when keyboard interaction closes the current menu surface.

paragraphs: Use `ui-menu` when you want a ready-made trigger plus overlay. Use `ui-menu-list` directly only when you genuinely need to control the shell around the menu surface.
Keep production menus short. If one menu becomes a navigation system or a settings panel, it usually needs a different component.
